svg.text: Global Timer
I. Global Timer Overview
The Global Timer component provides timing capabilities. It supports configuring multiple
sets of static timer data. Timers run independently on different active entities.
Two timer types: Countdown (counts down from a set duration) and Stopwatch (counts up).
Two activation methods: (1) Reference timer set in entity component, activated on entity creation.
(2) Node graph execution node activates the timer when executed.
II. Timer Management Tool
Predefine all global timers via the Timer Management Tool. The Global Timer List
enumerates all predefined timers in the current stage. You can add new timers, set names
and IDs, and configure basic parameters.
Parameters: Global Timer Type (Countdown or Stopwatch), Duration in seconds (for countdown).
Rename or delete timers using the icon next to each timer entry.
III. Referencing Global Timers
Via UI Controls
Open the Interface Layout Management Tool and select Timer UI Controls.
Configure: Type (Stopwatch/Countdown), Specify Timer (select predefined timer),
Source Entity (stage entity or player entity — must have a Global Timer component).
If inactive, UI displays 00:00. When active, UI shows the current timer time.
Via Entity Component
Add the Global Timer component via Add Common Component. Then add a timer reference
by selecting from the predefined timer list. The timer activates when the entity is created.
IV. Node Graph Integration
Start Global Timer
Node Type: Execute
Activates a predefined global timer. Only timers defined in the management tool are valid.
The running entity serves as the carrier for the timer.
Stop Global Timer
Node Type: Execute
Stops a running global timer early.
Pause Global Timer
Node Type: Execute
Pauses a running global timer. Linked UI controls also pause their display.
Recover Global Timer
Node Type: Execute
Resumes a paused timer. UI control display resumes as well.
Increase Global Timer Value
Node Type: Execute
Adjusts a running timer's time. For countdown: positive adds remaining time, negative reduces.
For stopwatch: positive increases accumulated time, negative decreases it.
Additional Event & Query Nodes
When Global Timer Is Triggered (Event): Fires when a countdown timer reaches zero.
Get Current Global Timer Time (Query): Returns remaining time (countdown) or accumulated
time (stopwatch) as a Floating Point Number.
